questions.question: What is a Cartesian plane?
questions.correct_answer: A plane formed by two perpendicular number lines used to locate points
questions.explanation: It consists of the x-axis and y-axis which intersect at the origin.

questions.question: What are the coordinate axes?
questions.explanation: These two perpendicular lines form the coordinate plane.

questions.question: What is the origin in coordinate geometry?
questions.correct_answer: The point where the x-axis and y-axis intersect
questions.explanation: The coordinates of the origin are (0,0).

questions.question: What are the coordinates of the origin?
questions.explanation: The origin has zero distance from both axes.

questions.question: What is meant by the coordinates of a point?
questions.correct_answer: An ordered pair (x, y) representing the position of a point
questions.explanation: The x-coordinate shows distance from the y-axis and the y-coordinate shows distance from the x-axis.

questions.question: What is the x-coordinate also called?
questions.explanation: It represents the horizontal distance from the y-axis.

questions.question: What is the y-coordinate also called?
questions.explanation: It represents the vertical distance from the x-axis.

questions.question: How many quadrants are formed by the coordinate axes?
questions.explanation: The axes divide the plane into four equal regions.

questions.question: What are the signs of coordinates in the first quadrant?
questions.explanation: Both x and y coordinates are positive in the first quadrant.

questions.question: What are the coordinates of a point on the x-axis?
questions.explanation: Points on the x-axis have zero distance from the x-axis in the vertical direction.

notes.title: Cartesian Plane
notes.content: The Cartesian plane is formed by two perpendicular lines called the x-axis and y-axis used to represent coordinates.

notes.title: Origin
notes.content: The origin is the intersection of the x-axis and y-axis and its coordinates are (0,0).

notes.title: Coordinates of a Point
notes.content: A point is represented by an ordered pair (x, y) where x is the abscissa and y is the ordinate.

notes.title: Quadrants
notes.content: The coordinate axes divide the plane into four quadrants numbered I, II, III and IV in anticlockwise order.

notes.title: Points on Axes
notes.content: Points on the x-axis have coordinates (x,0) and points on the y-axis have coordinates (0,y).

facts.fact: The x-axis is horizontal and the y-axis is vertical.
The coordinate axes divide the plane into four quadrants.
In the first quadrant both coordinates are positive.
